prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Frontier-orbital (HOMO/LUMO) energies and gap (eV)
Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP Gaussian basis, density-fitted (tier “standard” ≡ B3LYP/def2-TZVP)
Geometry provenance: single-point on the user-supplied aniline geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz); no re-optimization (DFT//user-xyz)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software/backend: PySCF  (invoked via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill)
Convergence & warnings: SCF converged in 9 cycles; no warnings reported by the engine.
Live log: the run streamed to a file named frontier_orbitals_<timestamp>.out in the caller’s working directory (path was printed to the terminal at launch and can be tailed for full SCF details). Result JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmptyzs_trp/live.json

Results (DFT//user-xyz):
• HOMO = –5.553772 eV
• LUMO = –0.2489544 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.304817651371971 eV (Koopmans-style orbital gap; not an excitation energy)

The headline value above is reported exactly as returned by the chemkit frontier-orbitals skill. No engine warnings were emitted, and the integrity checker flagged the result as trustworthy.
